title: Indonesia Open: Sindhu, Ayush Exit; Arjun-Hariharan Reach Quarterfinals
indian_angle: Top Indian players PV Sindhu and Ayush Shetty faced early exits, while the men's doubles pair of MR Arjun and Dhruv Kapila advanced to the quarterfinals.
summary: PV Sindhu lost to World No. 1 An Se Young, and Ayush Shetty was defeated by Lee Cheuk Yiu in the Round of 16 at the Indonesia Open, but Arjun and Hariharan secured a spot in the quarterfinals.

title: Sports Ministry to Form NCAB on Gopichand Panel's Advice
indian_angle: This initiative, led by Indian badminton legend Pullela Gopichand, aims to professionalize and improve coaching standards across India.
summary: The Sports Ministry is moving forward with the formation of the National Coaching Academy Board (NCAB), based on recommendations from a task force headed by Pullela Gopichand, to enhance coaching in India.

title: Satwik-Chirag Withdraw From Indonesia Open Due to Injury
indian_angle: India's top men's doubles pair, Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty, had to pull out of the Indonesia Open, raising concerns about Rankireddy's recurring shoulder issue.
summary: India's star men's doubles pair, Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty, have withdrawn from the Indonesia Open 2026 after Rankireddy sustained a shoulder injury, a recurring problem for the player.

title: India Invites EOI for BWF World Championships 2026 Hosting
indian_angle: India is set to host the prestigious BWF World Championships 2026 in New Delhi, a significant opportunity for Indian badminton on the global stage.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has issued an Expression of Interest for hosting the BWF World Championships 2026 at the IG Stadium Complex in New Delhi, scheduled for August 17-23, 2026.

title: Satwik-Chirag Crowned Singapore Open Champions
indian_angle: India's top men's doubles pair, Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty, secured a major title by winning the Singapore Open, showcasing their world-class form.
summary: Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty clinched the Singapore Open title with a dominant performance in the final, featuring furious rallies and their signature powerful smashes.

title: The Four Eyes That Guide Satwik & Chirag's Success
indian_angle: This feature delves into the coaching and support system behind India's star men's doubles pair, Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty, highlighting their journey to success.
summary: An in-depth look at the crucial role played by the coaching staff and support team in maintaining the high performance and strategic focus of Indian badminton's celebrated duo, Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty.
